question: Calculate the difference in the percentage of members in New Jersey and Vermont.
gold_sql: SELECT CAST((SUM(CASE WHEN T2.state = 'New Jersey' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN T2.state = 'Vermont' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.member_id) AS diff FROM member AS T1 INNER JOIN zip_code AS T2 ON T2.zip_code = T1.zip

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.state = 'New Jersey' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*)) -
    (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.state = 'Vermont' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(*)) AS diff_percentage
FROM 
    member AS T1
JOIN 
    zip_code AS T2 ON T1.zip = T2.zip_code
WHERE 
    T1.position = 'Member';

SELECT 
    (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN m.state = 'New Jersey' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(m.position)) - 
    (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN m.state = 'Vermont' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(m.position)) AS state_difference_percentage
FROM 
    member m
JOIN 
    zip_code z ON m.zip = z.zip_code;

SELECT 
    (SUM(CASE WHEN T2.state = 'New Jersey' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*)) * 100 -
    (SUM(CASE WHEN T2.state = 'Vermont' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*)) * 100 AS diff_percentage
FROM 
    member AS T1
JOIN 
    zip_code AS T2 ON T1.zip = T2.zip_code
WHERE 
    T1.position = 'Member';

SELECT 
    ((SUM(CASE WHEN T2.state = 'New Jersey' AND T1.position = 'Member' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100 / COUNT(*)) -
     (SUM(CASE WHEN T2.state = 'Vermont' AND T1.position = 'Member' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100 / COUNT(*))) AS diff_percentage
FROM 
    member AS T1
JOIN 
    zip_code AS T2 ON T1.zip = T2.zip_code;

SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.state = 'New Jersey' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T2.position = 'Member' THEN T2.position END) 
- CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T1.state = 'Vermont' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T2.position = 'Member' THEN T2.position END) 
FROM zip_code AS T1 
JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.zip_code = T2.zip

JOIN yearmonth AS T3 ON T1.CustomerID = T3.CustomerID AND T1.Date LIKE '%' || T3.Date || '%'

gold_sql: SELECT CAST((SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0))) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'SME' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)), CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'LAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) , CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2013%', T2.Consumption, 0)) - SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) AS FLOAT) * 100 / SUM(IIF(T1.Segment = 'KAM' AND T2.Date LIKE '2012%', T2.Consumption, 0)) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID

attempts.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    c.Segment,
    MAX(((y13.Consumption - y12.Consumption) / y13.Consumption) * 100) AS MaxPercentageIncrease,
    MIN(((y13.Consumption - y12.Consumption) / y13.Consumption) * 100) AS MinPercentageIncrease
FROM 
    customers c
JOIN 
    yearmonth y12 ON c.CustomerID = y12.CustomerID AND strftime('%Y', y12.Date) = '2012'
JOIN 
    yearmonth y13 ON c.CustomerID = y13.CustomerID AND strftime('%Y', y13.Date) = '2013'
GROUP BY 
    c.Segment;

SELECT 
    Segment,
    AVG(((SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2013' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2012' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END))) / SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y', Date) = '2013' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END)) * 100 AS AvgPercentageChange
FROM 
    yearmonth
JOIN 
    customers ON yearmonth.CustomerID = customers.CustomerID
GROUP BY 
    Segment;

WITH ConsumptionData AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Segment,
        SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4) = '2013' THEN ym.Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS Consumption2013,
        SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4) = '2012' THEN ym.Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS Consumption2012
    FROM 
        yearmonth ym
    JOIN 
        customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
    WHERE 
        c.Currency = 'EUR'
    GROUP BY 
        c.Segment
)
SELECT 
    Segment,
    MAX((Consumption2013 - Consumption2012) / Consumption2013) AS MaxPercentageIncrease,
    MIN((Consumption2013 - Consumption2012) / Consumption2013) AS MinPercentageDecrease
FROM 
    ConsumptionData;

WITH BiggestIncrease AS (
    SELECT Segment, 
           ((SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption END) - SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2012%' THEN Consumption END)) / 
            SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption END)) * 100 AS PercentChange
    FROM yearmonth
    JOIN customers ON yearmonth.CustomerID = customers.CustomerID
    WHERE Date LIKE '2012%' OR Date LIKE '2013%'
    GROUP BY Segment
    ORDER BY PercentChange DESC LIMIT 1
),
SmallestDecrease AS (
    SELECT Segment, 
           ((SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption END) - SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2012%' THEN Consumption END)) / 
            SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption END)) * 100 AS PercentChange
    FROM yearmonth
    JOIN customers ON yearmonth.CustomerID = customers.CustomerID
    WHERE Date LIKE '2012%' OR Date LIKE '2013%'
    GROUP BY Segment
    ORDER BY PercentChange ASC LIMIT 1
)
SELECT * FROM BiggestIncrease UNION ALL SELECT * FROM SmallestDecrease;

SELECT 
    Segment, 
    ((SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) -
      SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2012%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END)) /
     SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END)) * 100 AS PercentageChange
FROM 
    yearmonth
JOIN 
    customers ON yearmonth.CustomerID = customers.CustomerID
GROUP BY 
    Segment;

WITH ConsumptionData AS (
    SELECT 
        CustomerID,
        SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2012%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS Consumption2012,
        SUM(CASE WHEN Date LIKE '2013%' THEN Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS Consumption2013
    FROM yearmonth
    GROUP BY CustomerID
),
PercentageIncrease AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Segment,
        ((cd.Consumption2013 - cd.Consumption2012) / cd.Consumption2013) * 100 AS PercentIncrease
    FROM ConsumptionData cd
    JOIN customers c ON cd.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
)
SELECT Segment, MAX(PercentIncrease) AS MaxIncrease, MIN(PercentIncrease) AS MinDecrease
FROM PercentageIncrease
GROUP BY Segment;

SELECT GasStationID, SUM(Amount * Price) as TotalRevenue FROM transactions_1k GROUP BY GasStationID ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC LIMIT 1;

WITH revenue_per_station AS (
    SELECT 
        GasStationID,
        SUM(Amount * Price) AS TotalRevenue
    FROM 
        transactions_1k
    GROUP BY 
        GasStationID
),
ranked_stations AS (
    SELECT 
        GasStationID,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC) AS Rank
    FROM 
        revenue_per_station
)
SELECT 
    gs.GasStationID,
    gs.Description
FROM 
    ranked_stations rs
JOIN 
    gasstations gs ON rs.GasStationID = gs.GasStationID
WHERE 
    rs.Rank = 1;

question: Who is the top spending customer and how much is the average price per single item purchased by this customer? What currency was being used?
gold_sql: SELECT T2.CustomerID, SUM(T2.Price / T2.Amount), T1.Currency FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN transactions_1k AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T2.CustomerID = ( SELECT CustomerID FROM yearmonth ORDER BY Consumption DESC LIMIT 1 ) GROUP BY T2.CustomerID, T1.Currency

FROM transactions_1k t 
JOIN customers c ON t.CustomerID = c.CustomerID 
GROUP BY t.CustomerID 
ORDER BY SUM(t.Price) DESC 
LIMIT 1

SELECT c.CustomerID, AVG(t.Price / t.Amount) AS avg_price_per_item, c.Currency 
FROM transactions_1k t 
JOIN customers c ON t.CustomerID = c.CustomerID 
WHERE t.Amount > 0 
GROUP BY c.CustomerID 
ORDER BY SUM(t.Price) DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT c.CustomerID, SUM(t.Amount) AS TotalSpent, AVG(t.Price / t.Amount) AS AveragePricePerItem, c.Currency 
FROM transactions_1k t 
JOIN customers c ON t.CustomerID = c.CustomerID 
WHERE t.Amount > 0 AND t.Price > 0 -- Ensure no division by zero
GROUP BY c.CustomerID 
ORDER BY TotalSpent DESC 
LIMIT 1;

SELECT c.Currency, SUM(t.Price) AS TotalPrice, SUM(t.Amount) AS TotalAmount, AVG(t.Price / t.Amount) AS AvgPricePerItem 
FROM transactions_1k t 
JOIN customers c ON t.CustomerID = c.CustomerID 
GROUP BY t.CustomerID 
ORDER BY SUM(t.Price) DESC 
LIMIT 1

FROM customers c 
JOIN transactions_1k t ON c.CustomerID = t.CustomerID 
GROUP BY c.CustomerID 
ORDER BY SUM(t.Amount) DESC 
LIMIT 1;

question: For all the people who paid more than 29.00 per unit of product id No.5. Give their consumption status in the August of 2012.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.Consumption FROM transactions_1k AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE T1.Price / T1.Amount > 29.00 AND T1.ProductID = 5 AND T2.Date = '201208'

JOIN products p ON t.ProductID = p.ProductID 
JOIN yearmonth y ON t.CustomerID = y.CustomerID 
WHERE t.Date LIKE '2012-08-%' AND t.Price / t.Amount > 29 AND p.ProductID = 5

SELECT y.Consumption 
FROM yearmonth AS y 
JOIN (
    SELECT t.CustomerID 
    FROM transactions_1k AS t 
    WHERE t.ProductID = 5 AND t.Date LIKE '2012-08%' AND (t.Price / t.Amount) > 29
) AS subquery ON y.CustomerID = subquery.CustomerID AND y.Date = '201208'

SELECT 
    ym.CustomerID,
    c.Segment,
    c.Currency
FROM 
    (
        SELECT DISTINCT t.CustomerID
        FROM 
            transactions_1k t
        WHERE 
            t.ProductID = 5 AND (CAST(t.Price AS REAL) / t.Amount) > 29
    ) subquery
JOIN 
    yearmonth ym ON subquery.CustomerID = ym.CustomerID
JOIN 
    customers c ON subquery.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
WHERE 
    ym.Date = '201208'

SELECT T1.Date ,  T1.Price / T1.Amount AS price_per_unit, T3.Consumption FROM transactions_1k AS T1 JOIN customers AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID JOIN yearmonth AS T3 ON T2.CustomerID = T3.CustomerID WHERE T1.ProductID = 5 AND T1.Price / T1.Amount > 29 AND strftime('%Y%m', T1.Date) = '201208'

FROM transactions_1k AS t1 
JOIN yearmonth AS t2 ON t1.CustomerID = t2.CustomerID

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN T2.Consumption >= 2000 THEN 'High'
        ELSE 'Low'
    END AS ConsumptionStatus
FROM 
    transactions_1k AS T1
JOIN 
    yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID AND T2.Date = '201208'
WHERE 
    T1.ProductID = 5 AND (T1.Price / T1.Amount) > 29.00

SELECT 
    t.CustomerID,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(t.Amount) > y.Consumption THEN 'Over-spent'
        ELSE 'Within budget'
    END AS ConsumptionStatus
FROM 
    transactions_1k t
JOIN 
    yearmonth y ON t.CustomerID = y.CustomerID AND y.Date LIKE '201208%'
WHERE 
    t.ProductID = 5 AND (t.Price / t.Amount) > 29 AND t.Date LIKE '201208%'
GROUP BY 
    t.CustomerID;
